title: Parent Item date automation based on Subitem dates
excerpt: Automatically fill-in or adjust the parent Item start/end date columns based on the earliest and latest date for the relevant subitems.

posts.author: ABERNARD (ALEX BERNARD)
posts.cookedHtml: Automatically fill-in or adjust the parent Item start/end date columns based on the earliest and latest date for the relevant subitems.

posts.author: basdebruin (Bas de Bruin)
posts.cookedHtml: The app Rollup Subitems can do that. To define which subitems are relevant you can use filtering. Thereby the earliest and latest date of ONLY the relevant subitems will be put in the parent item (in a real monday column, not a mirror).

posts.author: ABERNARD (ALEX BERNARD)
posts.cookedHtml: Thanks, Bas. Our company is not currently reviewing or allowing additional apps, so we really need native solutions.

posts.author: i-jaglan (Sagar Jaglan)
posts.cookedHtml:
Hi Alex,

SubItems Pro can do exactly this with its Rollup Sync feature. Here’s how:

  1. Add the SubItems Pro Board View to your board
  2. Set up two rules:
    • Source: Subitem Date column → Function: EARLIEST → Target: Parent Start Date column
    • Source: Subitem Date column → Function: LATEST → Target: Parent End Date column
  3. Save Changes — and you’re done!

Once saved, it auto-syncs via webhooks — whenever a subitem date changes, the parent columns update automatically in
the background. No manual triggers needed.

If your subitems use Timeline columns instead, there’s also a SPAN function that writes the full range (earliest
start → latest end) directly into a parent Timeline column.

Setup takes about 30 seconds per board, and it writes to real monday.com columns (not mirrors).

Happy to help if you have any questions!
